title: Day 17 Quiz — RTK Query and Async Patterns
description: Verify promise chaining, API typing, query/mutation hooks, cache, and invalidations.

questions.prompt: RTK Query automatically deduplicates identical in-flight requests.
questions.explanation: Multiple subscribers to the same query share one request and cache entry.

questions.prompt: What hook reads cached server data in RTK Query?
questions.explanation: createApi generates typed query hooks per endpoint.

questions.prompt: What does invalidatesTags on a mutation do?

options.text: Marks cached queries stale so they refetch

questions.explanation: Tag invalidation triggers refetch of queries that provided matching tags.

questions.prompt: Which belong in configureStore when using RTK Query? Select all that apply.

options.text: Client slices like todos from Day 16

questions.explanation: RTK Query adds reducer and middleware; client slices coexist.

questions.prompt: Type a generic API wrapper as {{blank1}} where T is the data shape.
questions.explanation: Generic responses reuse one wrapper for many endpoints.

questions.prompt: What does .catch() receive if fetch fails network?
questions.explanation: Network errors reject the promise; catch receives the Error object.

questions.prompt: When is RTK Query preferred over hand-rolled fetch in useEffect?

options.text: Shared server data with caching, loading states, and mutations

questions.explanation: RTK Query shines for cached server state with lifecycle management.
invalidatesTags refreshes queries that provide the Product tag.

questions.sampleAnswer: providesTags labels cached query data so RTK Query knows what each entry represents. invalidatesTags on mutations marks those tagged entries stale, triggering refetch of affected queries.
questions.explanation: Tag system connects queries and mutations for automatic cache updates.

questions.prompt: Which states does useGetProductsQuery expose? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Query hooks expose data plus loading, error, and background fetch flags.
